data.content:
# Implementation Plan: Make setup CLI additive (Revision 5)

## Prior Rejection Gaps (all addressed)

1. Filtering detected agents: prior plans filtered out detected agents when base command already had registered variants. Fix: Always show all detected agents. Model menus show (current) markers. Dedup-aware _register_agent_entry handles re-selections.
2. PTY-driven interactive tests: prior plans only had writer/parser tests. Fix: Add test-setup-additive.sh and test-agent-wizard-additive.sh following test-install-copilot-setup.sh PTY pattern.
3. Full settings preservation: preserve backend.type, defaults.profileId, terminalLightTheme, not just agents/dispatch/pools/scope.
4. smol-toml compatibility: reader handles both shell-writer and smol-toml output.
5. Explicit REGISTERED_AGENTS=() reset removal in both scripts.
6. Fully additive dispatch: show existing mappings/pools with keep-current defaults.

## Step 1: Add _read_existing_settings() to setup.sh

Location: After KV helpers (~line 155). Pure-bash TOML parser reads $_AGENT_SETTINGS_FILE and populates KV store. Returns 0 if no file (fresh install unchanged).

State-machine with states: top, agents.<id>, actions, backend, defaults, scopeRefinement, pools, pools_aot.<step>. Handles top-level scalars, [agents.ID] sections, [actions], [backend], [defaults], [scopeRefinement] with multi-line triple-quote strings and smol-toml escaped strings, [pools] inline empty arrays, [[pools.STEP]] array-of-tables. ~90-100 lines.

## Step 2: Add identical _read_existing_settings() to agent-wizard.sh

Same logic for $SETTINGS_FILE variable. Must load ALL settings so the writer preserves them. ~90-100 lines.

## Step 3: Make _register_agent_entry() dedup-aware

Files: setup.sh:753-758, agent-wizard.sh:320-326. Check _append_unique before adding to REGISTERED_AGENTS. If exists, update metadata only. If new, append.

## Step 4: Remove REGISTERED_AGENTS=() resets

Delete setup.sh:912 and agent-wizard.sh:486 entirely. Array is pre-populated by Step 1. Agent wizard shows all detected agents (NO filtering by existing registrations). Model menus show all available models. Single-agent case only assigns action mappings when none exist yet.

Critical: does NOT filter FOUND_AGENTS. If codex-gpt-5 is registered and codex is detected, user sees full codex model menu and can add codex-gpt-5-2 as new variant.

## Step 5: Update _prompt_action_choice() to show current value

Files: setup.sh:718-738, agent-wizard.sh:285-305. Add current_agent parameter. Show (current) marker. Default selection is current agent. Enter keeps existing mapping.

## Step 6: Make _prompt_action_mappings() pass current values

Pass _kv_get ACTION_MAP value as current_agent to _prompt_action_choice.

## Step 7: Make _dispatch_wizard() show current state

When config exists: show current mode, offer Keep current as choice 1 (returns immediately). Fresh install: original 2-choice menu.

## Step 8: Make _prompt_single_pool() additive

Show current pool entries when they exist. Default to keep current. Option to reconfigure clears only THIS pool.

## Step 9: Make _defaults_wizard() use current values

Read from KV store (populated by reader). Use as prompt defaults instead of hardcoded 5/10.

## Step 10: Update _write_settings_toml() in both scripts

10a. backend.type from _kv_get BACKEND type "auto" (not hardcoded)
10b. defaults.profileId from _kv_get DEFAULTS profileId ""
10c. Add terminalLightTheme output
10d. Write pass-through agent fields (provider, flavor, version)
10e. agent-wizard.sh: full pool writer instead of empty-pools-only

## Step 11: Wire up entry points

setup.sh foolery_setup(): call _read_existing_settings before while loop, mark steps configured.
agent-wizard.sh maybe_agent_wizard(): call _read_existing_settings before scan prompt.

## Step 12: Tests

### A. Unit tests in setup-settings-writer.test.ts (7 new tests)

1. reader seeds agents into REGISTERED_AGENTS
2. reader seeds all scalar settings
3. reader seeds scope refinement prompt (multi-line)
4. reader seeds pool config
5. load-then-write round-trip preserves all settings
6. preserves existing agents when adding new ones
7. dedup on re-register

### B. PTY test: test-setup-additive.sh

Seed: 2 agents (claude-opus-4, codex-gpt-5), action mappings, backend.type=knots, defaults.profileId=guided, terminalLightTheme=true, maxConcurrentSessions=8, maxClaimsPerQueueType=15.

Run 1: Select Agents, scan, add claude-sonnet variant, Save & Exit. Assert: all existing agents preserved, new variant added, all mappings/scalars/backend/defaults unchanged.

Run 2: Select Dispatch, change one mapping, Save & Exit. Assert: all 3 agents present, changed mapping applied, everything else unchanged.

### C. PTY test: test-agent-wizard-additive.sh

Seed settings, run maybe_agent_wizard, add one variant. Assert existing agents + mappings + backend + defaults all preserved.

## Complexity: Medium

~250 lines new code, ~30 lines modified across both scripts. ~350 lines new tests. Risk: bash TOML parser (mitigated by round-trip tests). No new dependencies. Fresh installs unchanged.

## File Changes

| File | Type | Details |
|---|---|---|
| scripts/setup.sh | Modified | _read_existing_settings, dedup _register_agent_entry, remove reset, update wizards + writer |
| scripts/agent-wizard.sh | Modified | Same changes adapted for standalone wizard |
| src/lib/__tests__/setup-settings-writer.test.ts | Modified | 7 new test cases |
| scripts/test-setup-additive.sh | New | PTY-driven interactive additive test |
| scripts/test-agent-wizard-additive.sh | New | PTY-driven agent-wizard additive test |
| package.json | Modified | Add smoke test scripts |
